courses.title: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

notes: Physics General Concentration B.S. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer students must complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: PHYS 5A – Introduction to Physics I* PHYS 5B – Introduction to Physics II* PHYS 5C – Introduction to Physics III* MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 19B – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 23A – Vector Calculus *PHYS 5A, PHYS 5B, and PHYS 5C must be completed with a cumulative GPA of 2.70 or higher. Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to apply to these requirements. Winter Applicants Screening Policy Students applying for winter quarter admission are required to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, in addition to those required for fall transfer: PHYS 5D – Introduction to Physics IV MATH 23B – Vector Calculus See the "Recommended Preparation" and "Additional Major Preparation Courses" sections below for lists of courses approved to apply to these requirements. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete PHYS 5D – Introduction to Physics IV, or its articulated equivalent, to ensure timely graduation. Students who do not complete this preparatory course before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Additional Information for Transfer Prospective transfer students are also encouraged to complete the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before matriculation. For information on incorporating IGETC or UC Santa Cruz general education requirements into your pre-transfer academic plan, consult with your community college counselor. Please visit the Physics Department website to learn more about this major.

All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require the completion of specific preparatory courses and/or minimum GPA requirements. For detailed information about the screening requirements for this major, please refer to "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below. Additional preparatory coursework listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section are not required for admission, but are strongly recommended. Completion of these courses will help ensure timely progress toward degree completion. More information and a list of screening majors and non-screening majors may be found on the Admissions website.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.
